name: Kaliningrad Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the wealth of amber, for which the region is famous
the silver castle, standing for hospitality

description: Three horizontal stripes - red on top, narrow yellow in the middle, dark blue at the bottom - with a silver stylized medieval castle bearing Empress Elizabeth Petrovna's monogram in the upper hoist.
history: Adopted by law on 9 June 2006. The castle and monogram reference the brief Russian control of parts of the region under Empress Elizabeth Petrovna during the Seven Years' War.

features.symbolism: red for active human principle; yellow for the region's amber wealth; blue for the Baltic Sea and tranquility.

features.role: stylized medieval castle with monogram of Empress Elizabeth Petrovna
features.symbolism: a silver-and-black stylized medieval castle with open gates (hospitality) bearing the monogram of Empress Elizabeth Petrovna, recalling the brief Russian control of the region during the Seven Years' War.
